title: The Merchant with the Copper Ring
setting: A merchant has arrived in Greyhollow calling himself Vellis of the Eastern Roads. He is too charming — and his copper ring matches exactly the woven collar from the woods.

introVariations.text:
You bring the copper collar to Vellis's stall. His eyes flicker — only for a heartbeat — before his smile returns.

"A curious piece. May I see it?"

You confront him at his stall as dusk falls. The lanterns are lit. His goods glitter in their cases — but you see, beneath the velvet, ledgers of names and what was paid, what was promised.

"You're a thoughtful one," he says, smiling. "Most travelers don't read the fine print."

Two of his "guards" emerge from behind the wagon. They are not local men.

The wolf comes to the village edge that night. She does not enter. But she watches Vellis's wagon for hours, and the merchant himself does not come out until she is gone.

You confront him at his stall as dusk falls. The lanterns are lit. His goods glitter in their cases — but you see, beneath the velvet, ledgers of names and what was paid, what was promised.

"You're a thoughtful one," he says, smiling. "Most travelers don't read the fine print."

Two of his "guards" emerge from behind the wagon. They are not local men.

Vellis greets every villager warmly — including you. But you notice he never quite looks you in the eye. Something in him knows you have been to the woods.

You confront him at his stall as dusk falls. The lanterns are lit. His goods glitter in their cases — but you see, beneath the velvet, ledgers of names and what was paid, what was promised.

"You're a thoughtful one," he says, smiling. "Most travelers don't read the fine print."

Two of his "guards" emerge from behind the wagon. They are not local men.

Vellis offers you a healing tincture, free of charge. "For our brave hero," he says. The bottle is the wrong color. You don't drink it.

You confront him at his stall as dusk falls. The lanterns are lit. His goods glitter in their cases — but you see, beneath the velvet, ledgers of names and what was paid, what was promised.

"You're a thoughtful one," he says, smiling. "Most travelers don't read the fine print."

Two of his "guards" emerge from behind the wagon. They are not local men.

You confront Vellis at his stall as dusk falls. The lanterns are lit. His goods glitter in their cases — but you see, beneath the velvet, ledgers of names and what was paid, what was promised.

"You're a thoughtful one," he says, smiling. "Most travelers don't read the fine print."

Two of his "guards" emerge from behind the wagon. They are not local men.

fight.successText:
You take Vellis alive, though his guards do not survive the fight. From his wagon you recover ledgers, contracts, and a small iron-bound chest.

Inside the chest: dozens of copper rings, each etched with a different rune. And a journal, half-finished, in a hand that is not his own.
fight.failureText:
You wake outside the village walls. Vellis is gone. His wagon is gone. So is half the elder's coin and the blacksmith's wife's silver.

Whatever he was doing here, he is doing it elsewhere now.

charm.successText:
You don't draw your blade. Instead you ask him about the names in his ledger. The dates. The amounts. You ask, gently, what he is buying with all this coin.

He says nothing for a long moment. Then he laughs — a real laugh, a tired one. He tells you everything. He gives you a copper ring "for your trouble" and asks only that you let him leave by morning.

He is gone before sunrise. The villagers wake up missing nothing.
charm.failureText:
Vellis's smile sharpens. "Oh, you ARE clever. But cleverness is cheap."

He gestures, and his guards close in.

flee.successText:
You walk away from his stall without buying anything. You warn the elder. By the time the villagers gather, Vellis is gone — and so is much of his stock.

Whatever he was doing, you didn't stop it. But you did not feed it, either.
flee.failureText:
His guards block your retreat. One catches your arm and twists it hard. The other strikes from behind.

"You don't get to walk away yet," Vellis says.

chronicles.W3_FOUGHT: You took Vellis alive. From his wagon: ledgers, contracts, an iron-bound chest of copper rings — and a journal in a hand that was not his own.
chronicles.W3_CHARMED: You read the names in his ledger to him until he laughed, tired and honest. He left a copper ring on the counter and was gone before sunrise.
chronicles.W3_FLED: You walked from his stall and warned the elder. Vellis vanished by morning. Whatever he was doing, you didn't stop it — but you didn't feed it either.
chronicles.W3_LOST: You woke outside the village walls. Vellis was gone, and so was half the elder's coin and the blacksmith's silver.
